     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Reporting Dashboard (R13-S3)
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// the rollout: Full reporting dashboard for eval infrastructure.
     5	// Aggregates metrics per sprint/eval-run, per-channel views,
     6	// Trend analysis, and formatted report output.
     7	//
     8	// Uses: eval-db.ts singleton, eval_metric_snapshots table,
     9	// Eval_channel_results table.
    10	//
    11	// Design:
    12	//   - Read-only queries against eval DB (no writes).
    13	//   - Sprint labels inferred from metadata JSON or eval_run_id grouping.
    14	//   - Pure aggregation logic; DB access isolated to query functions.
    15	// External packages
    16	import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';
    17	
    18	// Internal modules
    19	import { getEvalDb, initEvalDb } from './eval-db.js';
    20	
    21	// Feature catalog: Reporting dashboard (eval_reporting_dashboard)
    22	
    23	
    24	// Configurable limit prevents silent data loss at scale (was hardcoded 1000)
    25	const DASHBOARD_ROW_LIMIT = Math.max(1, parseInt(process.env.SPECKIT_DASHBOARD_LIMIT ?? '10000', 10) || 10000);
    26	
    27	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    28	   1. TYPES
    29	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    30	
    31	/** Configuration for dashboard report generation. */
    32	export interface ReportConfig {
    33	  /** Filter to specific sprint labels (matched against metadata). */
    34	  sprintFilter?: string[];
    35	  /** Filter to specific channels (e.g. 'vector', 'bm25', 'fts', 'graph', 'trigger'). */
    36	  channelFilter?: string[];
    37	  /** Filter to specific metric names. */
    38	  metricFilter?: string[];
    39	  /** Maximum number of sprint groups to include (most recent first). */
    40	  limit?: number;
    41	}
    42	
    43	/** Summary statistics for a single metric. */
    44	export interface MetricSummary {
    45	  mean: number;
    46	  min: number;
    47	  max: number;
    48	  latest: number;
    49	  count: number;
    50	}
    51	
    52	/** Per-channel performance within a sprint. */
    53	export interface ChannelPerformance {
    54	  hitCount: number;
    55	  avgLatencyMs: number;
    56	  queryCount: number;
    57	}
    58	
    59	/** Report for a single sprint / eval-run group. */
    60	export interface SprintReport {
    61	  /** Sprint label (from metadata or eval_run_id). */
    62	  sprint: string;
    63	  /** Number of eval runs in this sprint group. */
    64	  evalRunCount: number;
    65	  /** Eval run IDs included in this sprint group. */
    66	  evalRunIds: number[];
    67	  /** Per-metric summary statistics. */
    68	  metrics: Record<string, MetricSummary>;
    69	  /** Per-metric, per-channel summary statistics derived from channel-tagged snapshots. */
    70	  channelMetrics: Record<string, Record<string, MetricSummary>>;
    71	  /** Per-channel performance data. */
    72	  channels: Record<string, ChannelPerformance>;
    73	  /** Earliest timestamp in this sprint group. */
    74	  firstSeen: string;
    75	  /** Latest timestamp in this sprint group. */
    76	  lastSeen: string;
    77	}
    78	
    79	/** A single trend data point comparing consecutive runs. */
    80	export interface TrendEntry {
    81	  /** Metric name. */
    82	  metric: string;
    83	  /** Previous value. */
    84	  previous: number;
    85	  /** Current value. */
    86	  current: number;
    87	  /** Absolute delta (current - previous). */
    88	  delta: number;
    89	  /** Percentage change. */
    90	  percentChange: number;
    91	  /** Direction indicator. */
    92	  direction: 'improved' | 'regressed' | 'unchanged';
    93	  /** Sprint/run label for the current value. */
    94	  currentLabel: string;
    95	  /** Sprint/run label for the previous value. */
    96	  previousLabel: string;
    97	}
    98	
    99	/** Complete dashboard report. */
   100	export interface DashboardReport {
   101	  /** ISO timestamp when report was generated. */
   102	  generatedAt: string;
   103	  /** Total eval runs included in this report after filters/limit. */
   104	  totalEvalRuns: number;
   105	  /** Total metric snapshots included in this report after filters/limit. */
   106	  totalSnapshots: number;
   107	  /** Per-sprint reports. */
   108	  sprints: SprintReport[];
   109	  /** Trend entries showing improvement/regression across runs. */
   110	  trends: TrendEntry[];
   111	  /** Human-readable summary paragraph. */
   112	  summary: string;
   113	}
   114	
   115	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   116	   2. INTERNAL HELPERS — DB QUERIES
   117	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   118	
   119	/**
   120	 * Lazy DB accessor. Initializes eval DB if needed.
   121	 * Safe to call repeatedly (initEvalDb is idempotent).
   122	 */
   123	// H18 FIX: Use existing eval DB singleton first to avoid silently switching
   124	// away from a non-default/test eval DB when generating a dashboard.
   125	function getDb(): Database.Database {
   126	  try { return getEvalDb(); } catch (_: unknown) { /* not yet initialized */ }
   127	  return initEvalDb();
   128	}
   129	
   130	/** Row shape from eval_metric_snapshots. */
   131	interface SnapshotRow {
   132	  id: number;
   133	  eval_run_id: number;
   134	  metric_name: string;
   135	  metric_value: number;
   136	  channel: string | null;
   137	  query_count: number | null;
   138	  metadata: string | null;
   139	  created_at: string;
   140	}
   141	
   142	/** Aggregated per-run/per-channel row shape from eval_channel_results. */
   143	interface ChannelResultRow {
   144	  eval_run_id: number;
   145	  channel: string;
   146	  hit_count: number;
   147	  latency_ms: number | null;
   148	  query_count: number;
   149	}
   150	
   151	/**
   152	 * Extract sprint label from a metadata JSON string.
   153	 * Looks for "sprint" key in the JSON. Returns null if not found.
   154	 */
   155	function extractSprintFromMetadata(metadata: string | null): string | null {
   156	  if (!metadata) return null;
   157	  try {
   158	    const parsed = JSON.parse(metadata);
   159	    if (parsed.sprint !== undefined && parsed.sprint !== null) {
   160	      return String(parsed.sprint);
   161	    }
   162	    if (parsed.sprintLabel) {
   163	      return String(parsed.sprintLabel);
   164	    }
   165	    return null;
   166	  } catch (_error: unknown) {
   167	    return null;
   168	  }
   169	}
   170	
   171	/**
   172	 * Determine if a metric is "higher is better" (true) or "lower is better" (false).
   173	 * Used for trend direction interpretation.
   174	 */
   175	function isHigherBetter(metricName: string): boolean {
   176	  // Metrics where lower values indicate better performance.
   177	  // Checked via prefix match on lowercase to handle variants
   178	  // (e.g. inversion_rate, inversion-rate, latency_ms).
   179	  const lowerName = metricName.toLowerCase();
   180	  const lowerIsBetterPrefixes = ['inversion', 'latency'];
   181	  return !lowerIsBetterPrefixes.some((prefix) => lowerName.startsWith(prefix));
   182	}
   183	
   184	/**
   185	 * Query all metric snapshots, optionally filtered.
   186	 */
   187	function queryMetricSnapshots(
   188	  db: Database.Database,
   189	  config: ReportConfig,
   190	): SnapshotRow[] {
   191	  let sql = `SELECT id, eval_run_id, metric_name, metric_value, channel, query_count, metadata, created_at
   192	             FROM eval_metric_snapshots
   193	             WHERE 1=1`;
   194	  const params: unknown[] = [];
   195	
   196	  if (config.metricFilter && config.metricFilter.length > 0) {
   197	    const placeholders = config.metricFilter.map(() => '?').join(', ');
   198	    sql += ` AND metric_name IN (${placeholders})`;
   199	    params.push(...config.metricFilter);
   200	  }
   201	
   202	  if (config.channelFilter && config.channelFilter.length > 0) {
   203	    const placeholders = config.channelFilter.map(() => '?').join(', ');
   204	    sql += ` AND (channel IN (${placeholders}) OR channel IS NULL)`;
   205	    params.push(...config.channelFilter);
   206	  }
   207	
   208	  sql += ` ORDER BY created_at DESC`;
   209	
   210	  const rows = db.prepare(sql).all(...params) as SnapshotRow[];
   211	
   212	  if (!config.sprintFilter || config.sprintFilter.length === 0) {
   213	    return rows.slice(0, DASHBOARD_ROW_LIMIT);
   214	  }
   215	
   216	  const filteredRows = rows.filter((snap) => {
   217	    const sprintFromMeta = extractSprintFromMetadata(snap.metadata);
   218	    const label = sprintFromMeta ?? `run-${snap.eval_run_id}`;
   219	    return config.sprintFilter!.some((filter) => label.toLowerCase().includes(filter.toLowerCase()));
   220	  });
   221	
   222	  // Apply the row safeguard after sprint filtering so matching older rows are not dropped prematurely.
   223	  return filteredRows.slice(0, DASHBOARD_ROW_LIMIT);
   224	}
   225	
   226	/**
   227	 * Query grouped channel results for specific eval_run_ids.
   228	 * Aggregating in SQL keeps channel coverage complete for the included runs
   229	 * without materializing every per-query row in memory.
   230	 */
   231	function queryChannelResults(
   232	  db: Database.Database,
   233	  evalRunIds: number[],
   234	  channelFilter?: string[],
   235	): ChannelResultRow[] {
   236	  if (evalRunIds.length === 0) return [];
   237	
   238	  const runPlaceholders = evalRunIds.map(() => '?').join(', ');
   239	  let sql = `SELECT
   240	               eval_run_id,
   241	               channel,
   242	               SUM(hit_count) AS hit_count,
   243	               AVG(latency_ms) AS latency_ms,
   244	               COUNT(*) AS query_count
   245	             FROM eval_channel_results
   246	             WHERE eval_run_id IN (${runPlaceholders})`;
   247	  const params: unknown[] = [...evalRunIds];
   248	
   249	  if (channelFilter && channelFilter.length > 0) {
   250	    const chPlaceholders = channelFilter.map(() => '?').join(', ');
   251	    sql += ` AND channel IN (${chPlaceholders})`;
   252	    params.push(...channelFilter);
   253	  }
   254	
   255	  sql += ` GROUP BY eval_run_id, channel`;
   256	
   257	  return db.prepare(sql).all(...params) as ChannelResultRow[];
   258	}
   259	
   260	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   261	   3. AGGREGATION LOGIC
   262	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   263	
   264	/**
   265	 * Group snapshots by sprint label. Sprint label is derived from:
   266	 * 1. metadata JSON "sprint" or "sprintLabel" field
   267	 * 2. Fallback: "run-{eval_run_id}"
   268	 */
   269	function groupBySprint(
   270	  snapshots: SnapshotRow[],
   271	  sprintFilter?: string[],
   272	): Map<string, SnapshotRow[]> {
   273	  const groups = new Map<string, SnapshotRow[]>();
   274	
   275	  for (const snap of snapshots) {
   276	    const sprintFromMeta = extractSprintFromMetadata(snap.metadata);
   277	    const label = sprintFromMeta ?? `run-${snap.eval_run_id}`;
   278	
   279	    if (sprintFilter && sprintFilter.length > 0) {
   280	      if (!sprintFilter.some(f => label.toLowerCase().includes(f.toLowerCase()))) {
   281	        continue;
   282	      }
   283	    }
   284	
   285	    if (!groups.has(label)) {
   286	      groups.set(label, []);
   287	    }
   288	    // Groups.set(label, []) above ensures the group exists before push
   289	    groups.get(label)!.push(snap);
   290	  }
   291	
   292	  return groups;
   293	}
   294	
   295	function getSprintTimeBounds(snapshots: SnapshotRow[]): { firstSeen: string; lastSeen: string } {
   296	  const timestamps = snapshots.map((snapshot) => snapshot.created_at).filter(Boolean).sort();
   297	  return {
   298	    firstSeen: timestamps[0] ?? '',
   299	    lastSeen: timestamps[timestamps.length - 1] ?? '',
   300	  };
   301	}
   302	
   303	/**
   304	 * Compute summary statistics for a set of values.
   305	 */
   306	function computeMetricSummary(values: number[], latest: number): MetricSummary {
   307	  if (values.length === 0) {
   308	    return { mean: 0, min: 0, max: 0, latest: 0, count: 0 };
   309	  }
   310	  const sum = values.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
   311	  // Reduce avoids stack overflow on arrays >100K elements (spread pushes all onto call stack)
   312	  return {
   313	    mean: Math.round((sum / values.length) * 10000) / 10000,
   314	    min: Math.round(values.reduce((a, b) => Math.min(a, b), Infinity) * 10000) / 10000,
   315	    max: Math.round(values.reduce((a, b) => Math.max(a, b), -Infinity) * 10000) / 10000,
   316	    latest: Math.round(latest * 10000) / 10000,
   317	    count: values.length,
   318	  };
   319	}
   320

    31	
    32	
    33	/** Strip absolute paths, stack traces, and truncate for safe user-facing hints. */
    34	function sanitizeErrorForHint(msg: string): string {
    35	  return msg
    36	    .replace(/(^|[\s(])\/(?:[^/\n]+\/)*[^:\n)"'\]]+/g, (_match, prefix: string) => `${prefix}[path]`)
    37	    .replace(/(^|[\s(])[A-Za-z]:\\(?:[^\\\n]+\\)*[^:\n)"'\]]+/g, (_match, prefix: string) => `${prefix}[path]`)
    38	    .replace(/^[ \t]*at .+$/gm, '')            // strip stack trace lines
    39	    .replace(/\n{2,}/g, '\n')                   // collapse blank lines left by stripping
    40	    .trim()
    41	    .slice(0, 200);
    42	}
    43	
    44	/** Redact absolute paths: keep only project-relative portion or basename. */
    45	function redactPath(absolutePath: string): string {
    46	  const normalizedPath = toNormalizedPath(absolutePath);
    47	  if (normalizedPath.startsWith('.opencode/')) return normalizedPath;
    48	  if (normalizedPath.startsWith('specs/')) return normalizedPath;
    49	  const opencodeIdx = normalizedPath.indexOf('/.opencode/');
    50	  const specsIdx = normalizedPath.indexOf('/specs/');
    51	  if (opencodeIdx !== -1) return normalizedPath.slice(opencodeIdx + 1);
    52	  if (specsIdx !== -1) return normalizedPath.slice(specsIdx + 1);
    53	  // Fallback: basename only
    54	  const lastSlash = normalizedPath.lastIndexOf('/');
    55	  return lastSlash !== -1 ? normalizedPath.slice(lastSlash + 1) : normalizedPath;
    56	}
    57	
    58	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    59	   CONSTANTS
    60	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    61	
    62	// Read version from package.json at module load time using ESM-relative paths.
    63	// WHY try-catch: if package.json is missing or malformed, the server should still start
    64	const SERVER_VERSION: string = (() => {
    65	  const packageCandidates = [
    66	    resolve(import.meta.dirname, '../package.json'),
    67	    resolve(import.meta.dirname, '../../package.json'),
    68	  ];
    69	
    70	  try {
    71	    for (const candidate of packageCandidates) {
    72	      if (!existsSync(candidate)) {
    73	        continue;
    74	      }
    75	      const pkg = JSON.parse(readFileSync(candidate, 'utf-8'));
    76	      if (pkg?.version) {
    77	        return pkg.version;
    78	      }
    79	    }
    80	    return 'unknown';
    81	  } catch (_error: unknown) {
    82	    return 'unknown';
    83	  }
    84	})();
    85	
    86	const DIVERGENT_ALIAS_REPORT_MODE = 'divergent_aliases';
    87	const DEFAULT_DIVERGENT_ALIAS_LIMIT = 20;
    88	const MAX_DIVERGENT_ALIAS_LIMIT = 200;
    89	const DOT_OPENCODE_SPECS_SEGMENT = '/.opencode/specs/';
    90	const SPECS_SEGMENT = '/specs/';
    91	
    92	interface AliasConflictDbRow {
    93	  file_path: string;
    94	  content_hash: string | null;
    95	  spec_folder?: string | null;
    96	}
    97	
    98	interface DivergentAliasVariant {
    99	  filePath: string;
   100	  contentHash: string | null;
   101	}
   102	
   103	interface DivergentAliasGroup {
   104	  normalizedPath: string;
   105	  specFolders: string[];
   106	  distinctHashCount: number;
   107	  variants: DivergentAliasVariant[];
   108	}
   109	
   110	interface DivergentAliasBucket {
   111	  hasDotOpencodeVariant: boolean;
   112	  hasSpecsVariant: boolean;
   113	  variants: Map<string, string | null>;
   114	  hashes: Set<string>;
   115	  specFolders: Set<string>;
   116	}
   117	
   118	function toNormalizedPath(filePath: string): string {
   119	  return filePath.replace(/\\/g, '/');
   120	}
   121	
   122	function toSpecAliasKey(filePath: string): string {
   123	  const normalizedPath = toNormalizedPath(filePath);
   124	  return normalizedPath
   125	    .replace(/\/\.opencode\/specs\//g, '/specs/')
   126	    .replace(/^\.opencode\/specs\//, 'specs/');
   127	}
   128	
   129	function isSpecsAliasPath(filePath: string): boolean {
   130	  const normalizedPath = toNormalizedPath(filePath);
   131	  return (
   132	    normalizedPath.includes(DOT_OPENCODE_SPECS_SEGMENT) ||
   133	    normalizedPath.includes(SPECS_SEGMENT) ||
   134	    normalizedPath.startsWith('.opencode/specs/') ||
   135	    normalizedPath.startsWith('specs/')
   136	  );
   137	}
   138	
   139	function isDotOpencodeVariantPath(normalizedPath: string): boolean {
   140	  return normalizedPath.includes(DOT_OPENCODE_SPECS_SEGMENT) || normalizedPath.startsWith('.opencode/specs/');
   141	}
   142	
   143	function isSpecsVariantPath(normalizedPath: string): boolean {
   144	  if (isDotOpencodeVariantPath(normalizedPath)) {
   145	    return false;
   146	  }
   147	  return normalizedPath.includes(SPECS_SEGMENT) || normalizedPath.startsWith('specs/');
   148	}
   149	
   150	function getDivergentAliasGroups(rows: AliasConflictDbRow[], limit: number): DivergentAliasGroup[] {
   151	  if (!rows.length) {
   152	    return [];
   153	  }
   154	
   155	  const buckets = new Map<string, DivergentAliasBucket>();
   156	
   157	  for (const row of rows) {
   158	    if (!row || typeof row.file_path !== 'string' || row.file_path.length === 0) {
   159	      continue;
   160	    }
   161	
   162	    const normalizedPath = toNormalizedPath(row.file_path);
   163	    const aliasKey = toSpecAliasKey(normalizedPath);
   164	    let bucket = buckets.get(aliasKey);
   165	    if (!bucket) {
   166	      bucket = {
   167	        hasDotOpencodeVariant: false,
   168	        hasSpecsVariant: false,
   169	        variants: new Map<string, string | null>(),
   170	        hashes: new Set<string>(),
   171	        specFolders: new Set<string>(),
   172	      };
   173	      buckets.set(aliasKey, bucket);
   174	    }
   175	
   176	    if (isDotOpencodeVariantPath(normalizedPath)) {
   177	      bucket.hasDotOpencodeVariant = true;
   178	    }
   179	    if (isSpecsVariantPath(normalizedPath)) {
   180	      bucket.hasSpecsVariant = true;
   181	    }
   182	
   183	    bucket.variants.set(normalizedPath, row.content_hash ?? null);
   184	    if (typeof row.content_hash === 'string' && row.content_hash.trim().length > 0) {
   185	      bucket.hashes.add(row.content_hash.trim());
   186	    }
   187	    if (typeof row.spec_folder === 'string' && row.spec_folder.length > 0) {
   188	      bucket.specFolders.add(row.spec_folder);
   189	    }
   190	  }
   191	
   192	  const groups: DivergentAliasGroup[] = [];
   193	  for (const [normalizedPath, bucket] of buckets.entries()) {
   194	    if (!bucket.hasDotOpencodeVariant || !bucket.hasSpecsVariant) {
   195	      continue;
   196	    }
   197	    if (bucket.variants.size < 2 || bucket.hashes.size < 2) {
   198	      continue;
   199	    }
   200	
   201	    const variants: DivergentAliasVariant[] = Array.from(bucket.variants.entries())
   202	      .sort(([pathA], [pathB]) => pathA.localeCompare(pathB))
   203	      .map(([filePath, contentHash]) => ({ filePath: redactPath(filePath), contentHash }));
   204	
   205	    groups.push({
   206	      normalizedPath: redactPath(normalizedPath),
   207	      // Fix F21 — redact specFolders to prevent path disclosure.
   208	      specFolders: Array.from(bucket.specFolders).sort().map(sf => redactPath(sf)),
   209	      distinctHashCount: bucket.hashes.size,
   210	      variants,
   211	    });
   212	  }
   213	
   214	  groups.sort((a, b) => a.normalizedPath.localeCompare(b.normalizedPath));
   215	  return groups.slice(0, limit);
   216	}

     2	// MODULE: Shadow Scoring with Holdout (REQ-D4-006)
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// Feature catalog: Shadow evaluation system for feedback learning
     5	// Compares would-have-changed rankings vs live rankings on a
     6	// holdout slice of queries. Tracks weekly improvement cycles and
     7	// gates promotion of learned signals to production.
     8	//
     9	// Feature flag: SPECKIT_SHADOW_FEEDBACK (default OFF)
    10	//
    11	// Key invariants:
    12	//   - Shadow-only: no live ranking columns are mutated
    13	//   - Holdout queries are deterministically selected via seed
    14	//   - Promotion requires 2+ consecutive weeks of stable improvement
    15	//   - All results logged to shadow_scoring_log for auditability
    16	import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';
    17	import { compareRanks } from './rank-metrics.js';
    18	import type { RankComparisonResult, RankedItem } from './rank-metrics.js';
    19	
    20	// Re-export rank-metrics for backward compatibility
    21	export { classifyDirection, computeKendallTau, computeNDCG, computeMRR, compareRanks } from './rank-metrics.js';
    22	export type { RankDirection, RankDelta, RankComparisonMetrics, RankComparisonResult, RankedItem } from './rank-metrics.js';
    23	
    24	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    25	   1. CONSTANTS
    26	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
    27	
    28	/** Default holdout percentage (20% of queries). */
    29	export const DEFAULT_HOLDOUT_PERCENT = 0.20;
    30	
    31	/** Number of consecutive improvement weeks required for promotion. */
    32	export const PROMOTION_THRESHOLD_WEEKS = 2;
    33	
    34	/** Standard evaluation window: 7 days in milliseconds. */
    35	export const EVALUATION_WINDOW_MS = 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;
    36	
    37	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    38	   2. TYPES
    39	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
    40	
    41	/** Row shape stored in shadow_scoring_log table. */
    42	export interface ShadowScoringLogRow {
    43	  id: number;
    44	  query_id: string;
    45	  result_id: string;
    46	  live_rank: number;
    47	  shadow_rank: number;
    48	  delta: number;
    49	  direction: string;
    50	  evaluated_at: number;
    51	  cycle_id: string;
    52	}
    53	
    54	/** Aggregated result for a weekly evaluation cycle. */
    55	export interface CycleResult {
    56	  cycleId: string;
    57	  evaluatedAt: number;
    58	  queryCount: number;
    59	  /** Mean NDCG delta across holdout queries. */
    60	  meanNdcgDelta: number;
    61	  /** Mean MRR delta across holdout queries. */
    62	  meanMrrDelta: number;
    63	  /** Mean Kendall tau across holdout queries. */
    64	  meanKendallTau: number;
    65	  /** Total improved / degraded / unchanged across all queries. */
    66	  totalImproved: number;
    67	  totalDegraded: number;
    68	  totalUnchanged: number;
    69	  /** Whether this cycle counts as an improvement (meanNdcgDelta >= 0). */
    70	  isImprovement: boolean;
    71	}
    72	
    73	/** Row shape stored in shadow_cycle_results table. */
    74	export interface CycleResultRow {
    75	  id: number;
    76	  cycle_id: string;
    77	  evaluated_at: number;
    78	  query_count: number;
    79	  mean_ndcg_delta: number;
    80	  mean_mrr_delta: number;
    81	  mean_kendall_tau: number;
    82	  total_improved: number;
    83	  total_degraded: number;
    84	  total_unchanged: number;
    85	  is_improvement: number; // 0 or 1
    86	}
    87	
    88	/** Promotion gate evaluation result. */
    89	export interface PromotionGateResult {
    90	  ready: boolean;
    91	  consecutiveWeeks: number;
    92	  recommendation: 'promote' | 'wait' | 'rollback';
    93	}
    94	
    95	/** Options for `selectHoldoutQueries`. */
    96	export interface HoldoutOptions {
    97	  /** Fraction of queries to hold out (default 0.20). */
    98	  holdoutPercent?: number;
    99	  /** Seed for deterministic selection. */
   100	  seed?: number;
   101	  /** Optional intent classes for representative coverage. */
   102	  intentClasses?: Map<string, string>;
   103	}
   104	
   105	/** Options for `runShadowEvaluation`. */
   106	export interface ShadowEvaluationOptions {
   107	  holdoutPercent?: number;
   108	  seed?: number;
   109	  cycleId?: string;
   110	  evaluatedAt?: number;
   111	  intentClasses?: Map<string, string>;
   112	}
   113

   124	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   125	   3. FEATURE FLAG
   126	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   127	
   128	/**
   129	 * Check whether shadow feedback evaluation is enabled.
   130	 * Default: TRUE (graduated, default ON). Set SPECKIT_SHADOW_FEEDBACK=false to disable.
   131	 */
   132	export function isShadowFeedbackEnabled(): boolean {
   133	  const val = process.env.SPECKIT_SHADOW_FEEDBACK?.toLowerCase().trim();
   134	  return val !== 'false' && val !== '0';
   135	}
   136	
   137	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   138	   4. SCHEMA SQL
   139	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   140	
   141	const SHADOW_SCORING_LOG_SCHEMA_SQL = `
   142	  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS shadow_scoring_log (
   143	    id           INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
   144	    query_id     TEXT NOT NULL,
   145	    result_id    TEXT NOT NULL,
   146	    live_rank    INTEGER NOT NULL,
   147	    shadow_rank  INTEGER NOT NULL,
   148	    delta        INTEGER NOT NULL,
   149	    direction    TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(direction IN ('improved','degraded','unchanged')),
   150	    evaluated_at INTEGER NOT NULL,
   151	    cycle_id     TEXT NOT NULL
   152	  )
   153	`;
   154	
   155	const SHADOW_SCORING_LOG_INDICES_SQL = `
   156	  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_shadow_log_query_id     ON shadow_scoring_log(query_id);
   157	  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_shadow_log_cycle_id     ON shadow_scoring_log(cycle_id);
   158	  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_shadow_log_evaluated_at ON shadow_scoring_log(evaluated_at);
   159	  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_shadow_log_direction    ON shadow_scoring_log(direction)
   160	`;
   161	
   162	const SHADOW_CYCLE_RESULTS_SCHEMA_SQL = `
   163	  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS shadow_cycle_results (
   164	    id               INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
   165	    cycle_id         TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
   166	    evaluated_at     INTEGER NOT NULL,
   167	    query_count      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   168	    mean_ndcg_delta  REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.0,
   169	    mean_mrr_delta   REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.0,
   170	    mean_kendall_tau REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.0,
   171	    total_improved   INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   172	    total_degraded   INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   173	    total_unchanged  INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   174	    is_improvement   INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK(is_improvement IN (0,1))
   175	  )
   176	`;
   177	
   178	const SHADOW_CYCLE_RESULTS_INDICES_SQL = `
   179	  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_shadow_cycle_evaluated_at ON shadow_cycle_results(evaluated_at)
   180	`;
   181	
   182	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   183	   5. INITIALIZATION
   184	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   185	
   186	/** Track which DB handles have had the shadow-scoring schema initialized. */
   187	const initializedDbs = new WeakSet<object>();
   188	
   189	/**
   190	 * Ensure the shadow_scoring_log and shadow_cycle_results tables exist.
   191	 * Idempotent — safe to call multiple times.
   192	 */
   193	export function initShadowScoringLog(db: Database.Database): void {
   194	  if (initializedDbs.has(db)) return;
   195	  db.exec(SHADOW_SCORING_LOG_SCHEMA_SQL);
   196	  db.exec(SHADOW_SCORING_LOG_INDICES_SQL);
   197	  db.exec(SHADOW_CYCLE_RESULTS_SCHEMA_SQL);
   198	  db.exec(SHADOW_CYCLE_RESULTS_INDICES_SQL);
   199	  initializedDbs.add(db);
   200	}
   201	
   202	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   203	   6. SEEDED PRNG
   204	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   205	
   206	/**
   207	 * Simple seeded PRNG (mulberry32).
   208	 * Returns a function that produces deterministic floats in [0, 1).
   209	 */
   210	export function seededRandom(seed: number): () => number {
   211	  let s = seed | 0;
   212	  return () => {
   213	    s = (s + 0x6D2B79F5) | 0;
   214	    let t = Math.imul(s ^ (s >>> 15), 1 | s);
   215	    t = (t + Math.imul(t ^ (t >>> 7), 61 | t)) ^ t;
   216	    return ((t ^ (t >>> 14)) >>> 0) / 4294967296;
   217	  };
   218	}
   219	
   220	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   221	   7. HOLDOUT SELECTION
   222	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   223	
   224	/**
   225	 * Select a holdout subset of query IDs for shadow evaluation.
   226	 *
   227	 * Uses a seeded PRNG for deterministic selection. When intentClasses
   228	 * is provided, applies stratified sampling to ensure representative
   229	 * coverage across intent categories.
   230	 *
   231	 * @param queryIds - Full set of available query IDs
   232	 * @param opts - Holdout configuration options
   233	 * @returns Array of selected holdout query IDs
   234	 */
   235	export function selectHoldoutQueries(
   236	  queryIds: string[],
   237	  opts: HoldoutOptions = {}
   238	): string[] {
   239	  const holdoutPercent = opts.holdoutPercent ?? DEFAULT_HOLDOUT_PERCENT;
   240	  const seed = opts.seed ?? 42;
   241	
   242	  if (queryIds.length === 0) return [];
   243	  if (holdoutPercent <= 0) return [];
   244	  if (holdoutPercent >= 1) return [...queryIds];
   245	
   246	  const targetCount = Math.max(1, Math.round(queryIds.length * holdoutPercent));
   247	
   248	  // When intent classes are provided, use stratified sampling
   249	  if (opts.intentClasses && opts.intentClasses.size > 0) {
   250	    return stratifiedSample(queryIds, opts.intentClasses, targetCount, seed);
   251	  }
   252	
   253	  // Simple random sampling with deterministic seed
   254	  const rng = seededRandom(seed);
   255	  const tagged = queryIds.map(id => ({ id, sortKey: rng() }));
   256	  tagged.sort((a, b) => a.sortKey - b.sortKey);
   257	  return tagged.slice(0, targetCount).map(t => t.id);
   258	}
   259	
   260	/**
   261	 * Stratified sampling: ensures proportional representation of intent classes.
   262	 */
   263	function stratifiedSample(
   264	  queryIds: string[],
   265	  intentClasses: Map<string, string>,
   266	  targetCount: number,
   267	  seed: number
   268	): string[] {
   269	  const rng = seededRandom(seed);
   270	
   271	  // Group by intent class
   272	  const buckets = new Map<string, string[]>();
   273	  const unclassified: string[] = [];
   274	
   275	  for (const qid of queryIds) {
   276	    const intentClass = intentClasses.get(qid);
   277	    if (intentClass) {
   278	      let bucket = buckets.get(intentClass);
   279	      if (!bucket) {
   280	        bucket = [];
   281	        buckets.set(intentClass, bucket);
   282	      }
   283	      bucket.push(qid);
   284	    } else {
   285	      unclassified.push(qid);
   286	    }
   287	  }
   288	
   289	  // Add unclassified as a virtual bucket
   290	  if (unclassified.length > 0) {
   291	    buckets.set('__unclassified__', unclassified);
   292	  }
   293	
   294	  const selected: string[] = [];
   295	  const totalItems = queryIds.length;
   296	
   297	  // Proportional allocation per bucket
   298	  for (const [, bucket] of buckets) {
   299	    const proportion = bucket.length / totalItems;
   300	    const bucketTarget = Math.max(1, Math.round(targetCount * proportion));
   301	    const shuffled = bucket.map(id => ({ id, sortKey: rng() }));
   302	    shuffled.sort((a, b) => a.sortKey - b.sortKey);
   303	    for (let i = 0; i < Math.min(bucketTarget, shuffled.length); i++) {
   304	      selected.push(shuffled[i].id);
   305	    }
   306	  }
   307	
   308	  // Trim or pad to target count
   309	  if (selected.length > targetCount) {
   310	    return selected.slice(0, targetCount);
   311	  }
   312	
   313	  return selected;
   314	}
   315	
   316	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   317	   8. RANK DELTA LOGGING
   318	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   319	
   320	/**

    28	import type { UpdateArgs } from './memory-crud-types.js';
    29	
    30	// Feature catalog: Memory metadata update (memory_update)
    31	// Feature catalog: Validation feedback (memory_validate)

    33	// Feature catalog: Per-memory history log
    34	
    35	
    36	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    38	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    39	
    40	/** Handle memory_update tool -- updates metadata fields and optionally regenerates embeddings. */
    41	async function handleMemoryUpdate(args: UpdateArgs): Promise<MCPResponse> {
    42	  // A7-P2-1: Generate requestId for incident correlation in error responses
    43	  const requestId = randomUUID();
    44	  await checkDatabaseUpdated();
    45	
    46	  const {
    47	    id,
    48	    title,
    49	    triggerPhrases,
    50	    importanceWeight,
    51	    importanceTier,
    52	    allowPartialUpdate = false,
    53	  } = args;
    54	
    55	  if (typeof id !== 'number') {
    56	    throw new MemoryError(ErrorCodes.MISSING_REQUIRED_PARAM, 'id is required', { param: 'id' });
    57	  }
    58	
    59	  if (importanceWeight !== undefined && (typeof importanceWeight !== 'number' || importanceWeight < 0 || importanceWeight > 1)) {
    60	    throw new MemoryError(
    61	      ErrorCodes.INVALID_PARAMETER,
    62	      'importanceWeight must be a number between 0 and 1',
    63	      { param: 'importanceWeight', value: importanceWeight }
    64	    );
    65	  }
    66	
    67	  if (importanceTier !== undefined && !isValidTier(importanceTier)) {
    68	    throw new MemoryError(
    69	      ErrorCodes.INVALID_PARAMETER,
    70	      `Invalid importance tier: ${importanceTier}. Valid tiers: ${VALID_TIERS.join(', ')}`,
    71	      { param: 'importanceTier', value: importanceTier }
    72	    );
    73	  }
    74	
    75	  const existing = vectorIndex.getMemory(id);
    76	  if (!existing) {
    77	    throw new MemoryError(ErrorCodes.FILE_NOT_FOUND, `Memory not found: ${id}`, { id });
    78	  }
    79	
    80	  const database = vectorIndex.getDb();
    81	  const priorSnapshot = getMemoryHashSnapshot(database, id);
    82	
    83	  const updateParams: UpdateMemoryParams = { id };
    84	  if (title !== undefined) updateParams.title = title;
    85	  if (triggerPhrases !== undefined) updateParams.triggerPhrases = triggerPhrases;
    86	  if (importanceWeight !== undefined) updateParams.importanceWeight = importanceWeight;
    87	  if (importanceTier !== undefined) updateParams.importanceTier = importanceTier;
    88	
    89	  let embeddingRegenerated = false;
    90	  let embeddingMarkedForReindex = false;
    91	  let embeddingStatusNeedsPendingWrite = false;
    92	  let mutationLedgerWarning: string | null = null;
    93	
    94	  if (title !== undefined && title !== existing.title) {
    95	    console.error(`[memory-update] Title changed, regenerating embedding for memory ${id} [requestId=${requestId}]`);
    96	    let newEmbedding: Float32Array | null = null;
    97	
    98	    try {
    99	      // Embed title + content_text, not title alone.
   100	      // This produces better semantic embeddings that capture the full memory context.
   101	      const contentText = existing.content_text || '';
   102	      const embeddingInput = contentText ? `${title}\n\n${contentText}` : title;
   103	      newEmbedding = await embeddings.generateDocumentEmbedding(embeddingInput);
   104	    } catch (err: unknown) {
   105	      const message = toErrorMessage(err);
   106	      if (allowPartialUpdate) {
   107	        console.warn(`[memory-update] Embedding regeneration failed, marking for re-index [requestId=${requestId}]: ${message}`);
   108	        embeddingStatusNeedsPendingWrite = true;
   109	        embeddingMarkedForReindex = true;
   110	      } else {
   111	        console.error(`[memory-update] Embedding regeneration failed, rolling back update [requestId=${requestId}]: ${message}`);
   112	        throw new MemoryError(
   113	          ErrorCodes.EMBEDDING_FAILED,
   114	          'Embedding regeneration failed, update rolled back. No changes were made.',
   115	          { originalError: message, memoryId: id }
   116	        );
   117	      }
   118	    }
   119	
   120	    if (newEmbedding) {
   121	      updateParams.embedding = newEmbedding;
   122	      embeddingRegenerated = true;
   123	    } else if (!embeddingMarkedForReindex) {
   124	      if (allowPartialUpdate) {
   125	        console.warn('[memory-update] Embedding returned null, marking for re-index');
   126	        embeddingStatusNeedsPendingWrite = true;
   127	        embeddingMarkedForReindex = true;
   128	      } else {
   129	        throw new MemoryError(
   130	          ErrorCodes.EMBEDDING_FAILED,
   131	          'Failed to regenerate embedding (null result), update rolled back. No changes were made.',
   132	          { memoryId: id }
   133	        );
   134	      }
   135	    }
   136	  }
   137	
   138	  // T2-5 transaction wrapper — wraps all synchronous mutation steps (DB update,
   139	  // Cache invalidation, BM25 re-index, ledger append) in a single transaction for atomicity.
   140	  // Embedding generation (async) runs before this block; its result feeds into updateParams.
   141	  const fields = Object.keys(updateParams).filter((key) => key !== 'id' && key !== 'embedding');
   142	
   143	  if (database) {
   144	    runInTransaction(database, () => {
   145	      if (embeddingStatusNeedsPendingWrite) {
   146	        vectorIndex.updateEmbeddingStatus(id, 'pending');
   147	      }
   148	
   149	      vectorIndex.updateMemory(updateParams);
   150	
   151	      // T2-6 — BM25 index stores title + trigger phrases; must re-index when either changes
   152	      // So keyword search reflects the updated content.
   153	      // T-05: BM25 re-index failure now rolls back the transaction when the index is operational.
   154	      // Infrastructure failures (BM25 not available, DB missing prepare) are non-fatal warnings.
   155	      if ((updateParams.title !== undefined || updateParams.triggerPhrases !== undefined) && bm25Index.isBm25Enabled()) {
   156	        try {
   157	          const bm25Idx = bm25Index.getIndex();
   158	          const row = database.prepare(
   159	            'SELECT title, content_text, trigger_phrases, file_path FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?'
   160	          ).get(id) as { title: string | null; content_text: string | null; trigger_phrases: string | null; file_path: string | null } | undefined;
   161	          if (row) {
   162	            const text = bm25Index.buildBm25DocumentText(row);
   163	            if (text.trim()) {
   164	              bm25Idx.addDocument(String(id), text);
   165	            }
   166	          }
   167	        } catch (e: unknown) {
   168	          const bm25ErrMsg = e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e);
   169	          // T-05 + P1-02 fix: Distinguish infrastructure failures from data failures.
   170	          // Infrastructure failures mean the BM25 subsystem is unavailable or torn down —
   171	          // These are non-fatal warnings. Data failures mean the index IS operational but rejected
   172	          // The input — those must re-throw to roll back the transaction.
   173	          const isBm25InfraFailure = (msg: string): boolean =>
   174	            msg.includes('not a function') ||
   175	            msg.includes('not initialized') ||
   176	            msg.includes('Cannot read properties') ||
   177	            msg.includes('is not defined') ||
   178	            msg.includes('database is closed') ||
   179	            msg.includes('no such table');
   180	          if (isBm25InfraFailure(bm25ErrMsg)) {
   181	            console.warn(`[memory-crud-update] BM25 infrastructure unavailable, skipping re-index [requestId=${requestId}]: ${bm25ErrMsg}`);
   182	          } else {
   183	            console.error(`[memory-crud-update] BM25 re-index failed, rolling back update [requestId=${requestId}]: ${bm25ErrMsg}`);
   184	            throw new Error(`BM25 re-index failed: ${bm25ErrMsg}`);
   185	          }
   186	        }
   187	      }
   188	
   189	      // T-05: Record UPDATE history after successful mutation
   190	      try {
   191	        recordHistory(
   192	          id, 'UPDATE',
   193	          existing.title ?? null,
   194	          updateParams.title ?? existing.title ?? null,
   195	          'mcp:memory_update'
   196	        );
   197	      } catch (_histErr: unknown) {
   198	        // History recording is best-effort
   199	      }
   200	
   201	      const ledgerRecorded = appendMutationLedgerSafe(database, {
   202	        mutationType: 'update',
   203	        reason: 'memory_update: metadata update',
   204	        priorHash: priorSnapshot?.content_hash ?? (existing.content_hash as string | null) ?? null,
   205	        newHash: mutationLedger.computeHash(JSON.stringify({
   206	          id,
   207	          title: updateParams.title ?? existing.title ?? null,
   208	          triggerPhrases: updateParams.triggerPhrases ?? null,
   209	          importanceWeight: updateParams.importanceWeight ?? null,
   210	          importanceTier: updateParams.importanceTier ?? null,
   211	        })),
   212	        linkedMemoryIds: [id],
   213	        decisionMeta: {
   214	          tool: 'memory_update',
   215	          fields,
   216	          embeddingRegenerated,
   217	          embeddingMarkedForReindex,
   218	          allowPartialUpdate,
   219	        },
   220	        actor: 'mcp:memory_update',
   221	      });
   222	      if (!ledgerRecorded) {
   223	        mutationLedgerWarning = 'Mutation ledger append failed; audit trail may be incomplete.';
   224	      }
   225	    });
   226	  } else {
   227	    // P1-021 — No database handle means we cannot guarantee transactional
   228	    // Consistency. Abort early rather than risk partial state.
   229	    console.warn('[memory-crud-update] No database handle, aborting update to prevent partial state');
   230	    return createMCPErrorResponse({
   231	      tool: 'memory_update',
   232	      error: `Memory ${id} update aborted: database unavailable`,
   233	      code: 'E_DB_UNAVAILABLE',
   234	      details: { updated: null, fields },
   235	      recovery: {
   236	        hint: 'Restart the MCP server or run memory_index_scan() to reinitialize the database',
   237	        actions: ['Restart the MCP server', 'Call memory_index_scan()'],
   238	        severity: 'error',
   239	      },
   240	    });
   241	  }
   242	
   243	  let postMutationHooks: import('./mutation-hooks.js').MutationHookResult;
   244	  try {
   245	    postMutationHooks = runPostMutationHooks('update', { memoryId: id });
   246	  } catch (hookError: unknown) {
   247	    const msg = hookError instanceof Error ? hookError.message : String(hookError);
   248	    postMutationHooks = {
   249	      latencyMs: 0, triggerCacheCleared: false,
   250	      constitutionalCacheCleared: false, toolCacheInvalidated: 0,
   251	      graphSignalsCacheCleared: false, coactivationCacheCleared: false,
   252	      errors: [msg],
   253	    };
   254	  }
   255	  const postMutationFeedback = buildMutationHookFeedback('update', postMutationHooks);
   256	
   257	  const summary = embeddingMarkedForReindex
   258	    ? `Memory ${id} updated (embedding pending re-index)`
   259	    : `Memory ${id} updated successfully`;
   260	
   261	  const hints: string[] = [];
   262	  if (embeddingMarkedForReindex) {
   263	    hints.push('Run memory_index_scan() to regenerate embeddings');
   264	  }
   265	  if (embeddingRegenerated) {
   266	    hints.push('Embedding regenerated - search results may differ');
   267	  }
   268	  if (mutationLedgerWarning) {
   269	    hints.push(mutationLedgerWarning);
   270	  }
   271	  hints.push(...postMutationFeedback.hints);
   272	
   273	  const data: Record<string, unknown> = {
   274	    updated: id,
   275	    fields,
   276	    embeddingRegenerated,
   277	    postMutationHooks: postMutationFeedback.data,
   278	  };
   279	
   280	  if (embeddingMarkedForReindex) {
   281	    data.warning = 'Embedding regeneration failed, memory marked for re-indexing';
   282	    data.embeddingStatus = 'pending';
   283	  }
   284	  if (mutationLedgerWarning) {
   285	    data.mutationLedgerWarning = mutationLedgerWarning;
   286	  }
   287	
   288	  return createMCPSuccessResponse({
   289	    tool: 'memory_update',
   290	    summary,
   291	    data,
   292	    hints,
   293	  });
   294	}
   295	
   296	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   297	   EXPORTS
   298	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   299	
   300	export { handleMemoryUpdate };

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Eval Metrics
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// Feature catalog: Core metric computation
     5	// T006a-e: Pure computation functions for 12 evaluation metrics
     6	// (7 core + 5 diagnostic). No DB access, no side effects.
     7	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     8	   1. TYPES
     9	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    10	
    11	/** A single retrieved result from a search query. */
    12	export interface EvalResult {
    13	  /** Unique memory identifier. */
    14	  memoryId: number;
    15	  /** Retrieval score assigned by the search system. */
    16	  score: number;
    17	  /** 1-based rank position in the result list. */
    18	  rank: number;
    19	}
    20	
    21	// H16 FIX: Use contiguous 1-based position within the topK slice, not external sparse rank.
    22	// External ranks can be non-contiguous after filtering/reranking, which would systematically
    23	// understate MRR/NDCG/MAP if used directly.
    24	function getRankAtIndex(_result: EvalResult, index: number): number {
    25	  return index + 1;
    26	}
    27	
    28	/** A single ground truth relevance judgment for a query-memory pair. */
    29	export interface GroundTruthEntry {
    30	  /** Identifier of the query this judgment belongs to. */
    31	  queryId: number;
    32	  /** Unique memory identifier. */
    33	  memoryId: number;
    34	  /**
    35	   * Relevance grade:
    36	   *   0 = not relevant
    37	   *   1 = partially relevant
    38	   *   2 = relevant
    39	   *   3 = highly relevant
    40	   */
    41	  relevance: number;
    42	  /** Optional importance tier (e.g. 'constitutional', 'critical', 'important', 'normal'). */
    43	  tier?: string;
    44	  /** Optional creation timestamp for cold-start detection. */
    45	  createdAt?: Date;
    46	}
    47	
    48	/** All computed metrics returned by computeAllMetrics(). */
    49	export interface AllMetrics {
    50	  mrr: number;
    51	  ndcg: number;
    52	  recall: number;
    53	  hitRate: number;
    54	  inversionRate: number;
    55	  constitutionalSurfacingRate: number;
    56	  importanceWeightedRecall: number;
    57	  coldStartDetectionRate: number;
    58	  precision: number;
    59	  f1: number;
    60	  map: number;
    61	  intentWeightedNdcg: number;
    62	}
    63	
    64	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    65	   2. INTERNAL HELPERS
    66	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    67	
    68	/**
    69	 * Build a lookup map from memoryId → relevance for fast access.
    70	 */
    71	function buildRelevanceMap(groundTruth: GroundTruthEntry[]): Map<number, number> {
    72	  const map = new Map<number, number>();
    73	  for (const entry of groundTruth) {
    74	    map.set(entry.memoryId, entry.relevance);
    75	  }
    76	  return map;
    77	}
    78	
    79	/**
    80	 * Build a lookup map from memoryId → GroundTruthEntry for full detail access.
    81	 */
    82	function buildGroundTruthMap(groundTruth: GroundTruthEntry[]): Map<number, GroundTruthEntry> {
    83	  const map = new Map<number, GroundTruthEntry>();
    84	  for (const entry of groundTruth) {
    85	    map.set(entry.memoryId, entry);
    86	  }
    87	  return map;
    88	}
    89	
    90	/**
    91	 * Sort results by rank ascending, return first k items.
    92	 */
    93	function topK(results: EvalResult[], k: number): EvalResult[] {
    94	  const safeK = Number.isFinite(k) ? Math.floor(k) : 0;
    95	  if (safeK <= 0) return [];
    96	  return [...results]
    97	    .sort((a, b) => a.rank - b.rank)
    98	    .slice(0, safeK);
    99	}
   100	
   101	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   102	   3. CORE METRICS
   103	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   104	
   105	/**
   106	 * Mean Reciprocal Rank at K (default K=5).
   107	 *
   108	 * For each query: find rank of first relevant result in top-K,
   109	 * take 1/rank. If no relevant result in top-K, contribution = 0.
   110	 * Average across all queries (treated as single query here since
   111	 * results are pre-filtered per call).
   112	 *
   113	 * @returns Value in [0, 1]. Returns 0 for empty inputs.
   114	 */
   115	export function computeMRR(
   116	  results: EvalResult[],
   117	  groundTruth: GroundTruthEntry[],
   118	  k: number = 5,
   119	): number {
   120	  if (results.length === 0 || groundTruth.length === 0) return 0;
   121	  if (groundTruth.every(entry => entry.relevance <= 0)) return 0;
   122	
   123	  const relevanceMap = buildRelevanceMap(groundTruth);
   124	  const topResults = topK(results, k);
   125	
   126	  for (let i = 0; i < topResults.length; i++) {
   127	    const rel = relevanceMap.get(topResults[i].memoryId) ?? 0;
   128	    if (rel > 0) {
   129	      return 1 / getRankAtIndex(topResults[i], i);
   130	    }
   131	  }
   132	
   133	  return 0;
   134	}
   135	
   136	/**
   137	 * Normalized Discounted Cumulative Gain at K (default K=10).
   138	 *
   139	 * DCG  = sum(relevance_i / log2(i+2)) for i in 0..K-1 (0-indexed)
   140	 * IDCG = DCG of ideal ranking (relevance sorted descending)
   141	 * NDCG = DCG / IDCG
   142	 *
   143	 * @returns Value in [0, 1]. Returns 0 for empty inputs or zero IDCG.
   144	 */
   145	export function computeNDCG(
   146	  results: EvalResult[],
   147	  groundTruth: GroundTruthEntry[],
   148	  k: number = 10,
   149	): number {
   150	  if (results.length === 0 || groundTruth.length === 0) return 0;
   151	  if (groundTruth.every(entry => entry.relevance <= 0)) return 0;
   152	
   153	  const relevanceMap = buildRelevanceMap(groundTruth);
   154	  const topResults = topK(results, k);
   155	
   156	  // Compute DCG
   157	  let dcg = 0;
   158	  for (let i = 0; i < topResults.length; i++) {
   159	    const rel = relevanceMap.get(topResults[i].memoryId) ?? 0;
   160	    dcg += rel / Math.log2(getRankAtIndex(topResults[i], i) + 1);
   161	  }
   162	
   163	  // Compute IDCG: ideal ordering of all ground truth relevances, top-K
   164	  const idealRelevances = [...groundTruth]
   165	    .map(e => e.relevance)
   166	    .sort((a, b) => b - a)
   167	    .slice(0, k);
   168	
   169	  let idcg = 0;
   170	  for (let i = 0; i < idealRelevances.length; i++) {
   171	    idcg += idealRelevances[i] / Math.log2(i + 2);
   172	  }
   173	
   174	  if (idcg === 0) return 0;
   175	
   176	  return Math.min(1, dcg / idcg);
   177	}
   178	
   179	/**
   180	 * Recall at K (default K=20).
   181	 *
   182	 * What fraction of all relevant memories (relevance > 0) in ground
   183	 * truth appear in the top-K results?
   184	 *
   185	 * @returns Value in [0, 1]. Returns 0 when no relevant items exist.
   186	 */
   187	export function computeRecall(
   188	  results: EvalResult[],
   189	  groundTruth: GroundTruthEntry[],
   190	  k: number = 20,
   191	): number {
   192	  if (results.length === 0 || groundTruth.length === 0) return 0;
   193	
   194	  const relevantIds = new Set(
   195	    groundTruth.filter(e => e.relevance > 0).map(e => e.memoryId),
   196	  );
   197	
   198	  if (relevantIds.size === 0) return 0;
   199	
   200	  const topResults = topK(results, k);
   201	  // F-28 — Deduplicate hits by memoryId to prevent Recall > 1.0
   202	  // When the same memory appears multiple times in results.
   203	  const seenIds = new Set<number>();
   204	  let hits = 0;
   205	  for (const r of topResults) {
   206	    if (relevantIds.has(r.memoryId) && !seenIds.has(r.memoryId)) {
   207	      hits++;
   208	      seenIds.add(r.memoryId);
   209	    }
   210	  }
   211	
   212	  return hits / relevantIds.size;
   213	}
   214	
   215	/**
   216	 * Compute Precision@K — fraction of retrieved results that are relevant.
   217	 * Precision = |relevant ∩ retrieved@K| / K
   218	 */
   219	export function computePrecision(
   220	  results: EvalResult[],
   221	  groundTruth: GroundTruthEntry[],
   222	  k: number = 20
   223	): number {
   224	  if (k <= 0 || results.length === 0 || groundTruth.length === 0) return 0;
   225	  const relevantIds = new Set(
   226	    groundTruth.filter(e => e.relevance > 0).map(e => e.memoryId),
   227	  );
   228	  const topResults = topK(results, k);
   229	  if (topResults.length === 0) return 0;
   230	  const seenIds = new Set<number>();
   231	  let hits = 0;
   232	  for (const r of topResults) {
   233	    if (seenIds.has(r.memoryId)) continue;
   234	    seenIds.add(r.memoryId);
   235	    if (relevantIds.has(r.memoryId)) hits++;
   236	  }
   237	  return hits / k;
   238	}
   239	
   240	/**
   241	 * Compute F1@K — harmonic mean of Precision@K and Recall@K.
   242	 * F1 = 2 * (P * R) / (P + R), or 0 if both are 0.
   243	 */
   244	export function computeF1(
   245	  results: EvalResult[],
   246	  groundTruth: GroundTruthEntry[],
   247	  k: number = 20
   248	): number {
   249	  if (k <= 0) return 0;
   250	  const p = computePrecision(results, groundTruth, k);
   251	  const r = computeRecall(results, groundTruth, k);
   252	  if (p + r === 0) return 0;
   253	  return 2 * (p * r) / (p + r);
   254	}
   255	
   256	/**
   257	 * Mean Average Precision (MAP).
   258	 *
   259	 * AP = (1 / |relevant|) × Σ(Precision@k × rel(k)) for k = 1..K
   260	 * where rel(k) is 1 if the k-th result is relevant, 0 otherwise.
   261	 *
   262	 * MAP is the mean of AP across queries (single query here since
   263	 * results are pre-filtered per call).
   264	 *
   265	 * @param results - Retrieved results for the query.
   266	 * @param groundTruth - Ground truth relevance judgments.
   267	 * @param k - Cutoff. Defaults to 20.
   268	 * @returns Value in [0, 1]. Returns 0 for empty inputs.
   269	 */
   270	export function computeMAP(
   271	  results: EvalResult[],
   272	  groundTruth: GroundTruthEntry[],
   273	  k: number = 20,
   274	): number {
   275	  if (results.length === 0 || groundTruth.length === 0) return 0;
   276	
   277	  const relevantIds = new Set(
   278	    groundTruth.filter(e => e.relevance > 0).map(e => e.memoryId),
   279	  );
   280	  if (relevantIds.size === 0) return 0;

   648	/** Handle memory_validate tool - records user validation feedback to adjust confidence */
   649	async function handleMemoryValidate(args: MemoryValidateArgs): Promise<MCPResponse> {
   650	  const startTime = Date.now();
   651	  await checkDatabaseUpdated();
   652	  const {
   653	    id,
   654	    wasUseful,
   655	    queryId,
   656	    queryTerms,
   657	    resultRank,
   658	    totalResultsShown,
   659	    searchMode,
   660	    intent,
   661	    sessionId,
   662	    notes,
   663	  } = args;
   664	
   665	  if (id === undefined || id === null) {
   666	    throw new Error('id is required');
   667	  }
   668	
   669	  if (typeof wasUseful !== 'boolean') {
   670	    throw new Error('wasUseful is required and must be a boolean');
   671	  }
   672	
   673	  const memoryId = parseMemoryId(id);
   674	
   675	  vectorIndex.initializeDb();
   676	  const database = requireDb();
   677	  const normalizedQueryId = typeof queryId === 'string' && queryId.trim().length > 0
   678	    ? queryId.trim()
   679	    : null;
   680	  const queryText = resolveValidationQueryText(database, normalizedQueryId ?? undefined);
   681	  const result: ValidationResult = confidenceTracker.recordValidation(database, memoryId, wasUseful);
   682	  try {
   683	    recordAdaptiveSignal(database, {
   684	      memoryId,
   685	      signalType: wasUseful ? 'outcome' : 'correction',
   686	      signalValue: 1,
   687	      query: queryText,
   688	      actor: sessionId ?? 'memory_validate',
   689	      metadata: {
   690	        queryId: normalizedQueryId,
   691	        queryText,
   692	        resultRank: typeof resultRank === 'number' ? resultRank : null,
   693	        totalResultsShown: typeof totalResultsShown === 'number' ? totalResultsShown : null,
   694	        intent: intent ?? null,
   695	      },
   696	    });
   697	  } catch (_error: unknown) {
   698	    // Adaptive signals are best-effort only
   699	  }
   700	
   701	  // T002a: Auto-promotion wiring on positive feedback.
   702	  let autoPromotion: {
   703	    attempted: boolean;
   704	    promoted: boolean;
   705	    previousTier?: string;
   706	    newTier?: string;
   707	    reason?: string;
   708	  } | null = null;
   709	
   710	  if (wasUseful) {
   711	    const promotionResult = executeAutoPromotion(database, memoryId);
   712	    autoPromotion = {
   713	      attempted: true,
   714	      promoted: promotionResult.promoted,
   715	      previousTier: promotionResult.previousTier,
   716	      newTier: promotionResult.newTier,
   717	      reason: promotionResult.reason,
   718	    };
   719	  }
   720	
   721	  // T002b: Negative-feedback confidence signal persistence for runtime scoring.
   722	  if (!wasUseful) {
   723	    recordNegativeFeedbackEvent(database, memoryId);
   724	  }
   725	
   726	  // T002 + T027a: Optional wiring from memory_validate to learned feedback + ground truth.
   727	  let learnedFeedback: {
   728	    attempted: boolean;
   729	    applied: boolean;
   730	    termsLearned: string[];
   731	    reason?: string;
   732	  } | null = null;
   733	  let groundTruthSelectionId: number | null = null;
   734	
   735	  if (wasUseful && typeof queryId === 'string' && queryId.trim().length > 0) {
   736	    groundTruthSelectionId = recordUserSelection(queryId, memoryId, {
   737	      searchMode,
   738	      intent,
   739	      selectedRank: resultRank,
   740	      totalResultsShown,
   741	      sessionId,
   742	      notes,
   743	    });
   744	
   745	    const normalizedTerms = Array.isArray(queryTerms)
   746	      ? queryTerms.filter((term) => typeof term === 'string' && term.trim().length > 0).map((term) => term.trim())
   747	      : [];
   748	
   749	    if (typeof resultRank === 'number' && Number.isFinite(resultRank) && resultRank > 0 && normalizedTerms.length > 0) {
   750	      const learnResult = recordSelection(queryId, memoryId, normalizedTerms, Math.floor(resultRank), database);
   751	      learnedFeedback = {
   752	        attempted: true,
   753	        applied: learnResult.applied,
   754	        termsLearned: learnResult.terms,
   755	        reason: learnResult.reason,
   756	      };
   757	    } else {
   758	      learnedFeedback = {
   759	        attempted: false,
   760	        applied: false,
   761	        termsLearned: [],
   762	        reason: 'missing_query_terms_or_rank',
   763	      };
   764	    }
   765	  }
   766	
   767	  const summary = wasUseful
   768	    ? `Positive validation recorded (confidence: ${result.confidence.toFixed(2)})`
   769	    : `Negative validation recorded (confidence: ${result.confidence.toFixed(2)})`;
   770	
   771	  const hints: string[] = [];
   772	  if (result.promotionEligible) {
   773	    hints.push('Memory eligible for promotion to critical tier');
   774	  }
   775	  if (!wasUseful && result.validationCount > 3) {
   776	    hints.push('Consider updating or deleting this memory if consistently unhelpful');
   777	  }
   778	
   779	  return createMCPSuccessResponse({
   780	    tool: 'memory_validate',
   781	    summary,
   782	    data: {
   783	      memoryId,
   784	      wasUseful: wasUseful,
   785	      confidence: result.confidence,
   786	      validationCount: result.validationCount,
   787	      positiveValidationCount: result.positiveValidationCount,
   788	      promotionEligible: result.promotionEligible,
   789	      autoPromotion,
   790	      learnedFeedback,
   791	      groundTruthSelectionId,
   792	    },
   793	    hints,
   794	    startTime: startTime
   795	  });
   796	}
   797

   800	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */

   804	  handleCheckpointList,
   805	  handleCheckpointRestore,
   806	  handleCheckpointDelete,
   807	  handleMemoryValidate,
   808	};

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/scoring/confidence-tracker.ts | sed -n '96,220p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
    96	/**
    97	 * Record a validation event for a memory and persist confidence counters.
    98	 *
    99	 * Stage 2 integration hooks:
   100	 * - This function updates `memory_index.confidence` and `validation_count`,
   101	 *   establishing durable feedback state for search-stage signal consumers.
   102	 * - For `wasUseful=false`, `handlers/checkpoints.ts` pairs this update with
   103	 *   `recordNegativeFeedbackEvent`, and Stage 2 (`search/pipeline/stage2-fusion.ts`)
   104	 *   reads those events via `getNegativeFeedbackStats` to apply demotion.
   105	 * - Positive validations can trigger auto-promotion (`importance_tier` changes),
   106	 *   which Stage 2 validation metadata scoring treats as a quality signal.
   107	 */
   108	export function recordValidation(db: Database, memoryId: number, wasUseful: boolean): ValidationResult {
   109	  try {
   110	    // Keep read/compute/write in one transaction so concurrent updates cannot drop validations.
   111	    return db.transaction(() => {
   112	      // Read first so this update derives from the latest persisted values.
   113	      const memory = db.prepare(`
   114	        SELECT confidence, validation_count, importance_tier FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?
   115	      `).get(memoryId) as MemoryConfidenceRow | undefined;
   116	
   117	      if (!memory) {
   118	        throw new Error(`Memory not found: ${memoryId}`);
   119	      }
   120	
   121	      const currentConfidence = memory.confidence ?? CONFIDENCE_BASE;
   122	      let newConfidence: number;
   123	
   124	      if (wasUseful) {
   125	        newConfidence = Math.min(currentConfidence + CONFIDENCE_POSITIVE_INCREMENT, CONFIDENCE_MAX);
   126	      } else {
   127	        newConfidence = Math.max(currentConfidence - CONFIDENCE_NEGATIVE_DECREMENT, CONFIDENCE_MIN);
   128	      }
   129	
   130	      const currentValidationCount = memory.validationCount ?? memory.validation_count ?? 0;
   131	      const newValidationCount = currentValidationCount + 1;
   132	
   133	      db.prepare(`
   134	        UPDATE memory_index
   135	        SET confidence = ?, validation_count = ?, updated_at = ?
   136	        WHERE id = ?
   137	      `).run(newConfidence, newValidationCount, new Date().toISOString(), memoryId);
   138	
   139	      const priorNegativeValidationCount = getNegativeValidationCount(db, memoryId);
   140	      const effectiveNegativeValidationCount = wasUseful
   141	        ? priorNegativeValidationCount
   142	        : priorNegativeValidationCount + 1;
   143	      const positiveValidationCount = resolvePositiveValidationCount(
   144	        newValidationCount,
   145	        effectiveNegativeValidationCount
   146	      );
   147	
   148	      // Report eligibility only; promotion is intentionally explicit and separate.
   149	      const promotionEligible = isPromotionEligible(
   150	        memory.importance_tier,
   151	        newConfidence,
   152	        positiveValidationCount
   153	      );
   154	
   155	      if (!wasUseful) {
   156	        const chunkId = memoryId;
   157	        console.warn('[confidence-tracker] negative feedback recorded', {
   158	          chunkId,
   159	          previousConfidence: currentConfidence,
   160	          newConfidence,
   161	          decrement: CONFIDENCE_NEGATIVE_DECREMENT,
   162	        });
   163	      }
   164	
   165	      return {
   166	        confidence: newConfidence,
   167	        validationCount: newValidationCount,
   168	        positiveValidationCount,
   169	        promotionEligible,
   170	        wasPromoted: false,
   171	      };
   172	    })();
   173	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   174	    // T-07: Replace success-shaped fallback with explicit failure signaling.
   175	    // Previously returned default values that looked like success, hiding DB errors
   176	    // From callers and allowing downstream side-effects to proceed on stale data.
   177	    console.error(`[confidence-tracker] recordValidation failed for memory ${memoryId}:`, error);
   178	    throw error;
   179	  }
   180	}
   181	
   182	/**
   183	 * Get current confidence score for a memory.
   184	 */
   185	export function getConfidenceScore(db: Database, memoryId: number): number {
   186	  try {
   187	    // DB op 3: SELECT
   188	    const memory = db.prepare(`
   189	      SELECT confidence FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?
   190	    `).get(memoryId) as { confidence?: number } | undefined;
   191	
   192	    if (!memory) {
   193	      throw new Error(`Memory not found: ${memoryId}`);
   194	    }
   195	
   196	    return memory.confidence ?? CONFIDENCE_BASE;
   197	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   198	    console.error(`[confidence-tracker] getConfidenceScore failed for memory ${memoryId}:`, error);
   199	    return CONFIDENCE_BASE;
   200	  }
   201	}
   202	
   203	/**
   204	 * Check if a memory is eligible for promotion to critical tier.
   205	 */
   206	export function checkPromotionEligible(db: Database, memoryId: number): boolean {
   207	  try {
   208	    const memory = db.prepare(`
   209	      SELECT confidence, validation_count, importance_tier FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?
   210	    `).get(memoryId) as MemoryConfidenceRow | undefined;
   211	
   212	    if (!memory) {
   213	      return false;
   214	    }
   215	
   216	    // Top-tier memories should not be re-promoted.
   217	    if (memory.importance_tier === 'critical' || memory.importance_tier === 'constitutional') {
   218	      return false;
   219	    }
   220

   839	
   840	/**
   841	 * Apply FSRS testing effect (strengthening write-back) for all accessed memories.
   842	 *
   843	 * Called only when `trackAccess` is true (P3-09 FIX: explicit opt-in to avoid
   844	 * unintended write side-effects during read-only searches).
   845	 *
   846	 * For each result, the current retrievability R(t) is computed from the stored
   847	 * stability and last_review fields, then `strengthenOnAccess` fires an FSRS
   848	 * GRADE_GOOD update — increasing stability proportional to how much the memory
   849	 * needed the review.
   850	 *
   851	 * Errors per-row are caught and logged; they do not abort the full set.
   852	 *
   853	 * @param db      - Active SQLite database connection
   854	 * @param results - Pipeline rows that were accessed
   855	 */
   856	function applyTestingEffect(
   857	  db: Database.Database,
   858	  results: PipelineRow[]
   859	): void {
   860	  if (!db || !Array.isArray(results) || results.length === 0) return;
   861	
   862	  for (const row of results) {
   863	    try {
   864	      const lastReview = (row.last_review as string | undefined | null) || (row.created_at as string | undefined);
   865	      if (!lastReview) continue;
   866	
   867	      const stability = typeof row.stability === 'number' && Number.isFinite(row.stability)
   868	        ? row.stability
   869	        : fsrsScheduler.DEFAULT_INITIAL_STABILITY;
   870	
   871	      const elapsedDays = fsrsScheduler.calculateElapsedDays(lastReview);
   872	      const currentR = fsrsScheduler.calculateRetrievability(
   873	        stability,
   874	        Math.max(0, elapsedDays)
   875	      );
   876	
   877	      strengthenOnAccess(db, row.id, currentR);
   878	    } catch (err: unknown) {
   879	      const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
   880	      console.warn(`[stage2-fusion] applyTestingEffect failed for id ${row.id}: ${message}`);
   881	    }
   882	  }
   883	}
   884	
   885	function recordAdaptiveAccessSignals(
   886	  db: Database.Database,
   887	  results: PipelineRow[],
   888	  query: string | undefined
   889	): void {
   890	  if (!db || !Array.isArray(results) || results.length === 0) return;
   891	
   892	  if (getAdaptiveMode() === 'disabled') return;
   893	
   894	  ensureAdaptiveTables(db);
   895	  const insertAdaptiveSignal = db.prepare(`
   896	    INSERT INTO adaptive_signal_events (memory_id, signal_type, signal_value, query, actor, metadata)
   897	    VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
   898	  `);
   899	  const writeAdaptiveSignals = db.transaction((accessedResults: PipelineRow[]) => {
   900	    for (const result of accessedResults) {
   901	      insertAdaptiveSignal.run(
   902	        result.id,
   903	        'access',
   904	        1.0,
   905	        query || '',
   906	        '',
   907	        '{}',
   908	      );
   909	    }
   910	  });
   911	
   912	  try {
   913	    writeAdaptiveSignals(results);
   914	  } catch (err: unknown) {

   916	    console.warn('[stage2-fusion] adaptive access signal write failed:', (err as Error)?.message ?? err);
   917	  }
   918	}
   919	
   920	// -- Main Stage 2 entry point --
   921	
   922	/**
   923	 * Execute Stage 2: Fusion + Signal Integration.
   924	 *
   925	 * This is the SINGLE authoritative point where all scoring signals are

   927	 * the architectural documentation (see types.ts Stage2 comment block).
   928	 *
   929	 * Signal application order (13 steps):

     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Eval Quality Proxy (T006G)
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// Feature catalog: Quality proxy formula
     5	// Automated quality proxy metric that correlates with manual
     6	// Quality assessment. Pure function, no DB access, no side effects.
     7	//
     8	// Formula:
     9	// QualityProxy = avgRelevance * 0.40 + topResult * 0.25
    10	// + countSaturation * 0.20 + latencyPenalty * 0.15
    11	//
    12	// All components normalised to [0, 1]. Output range: [0, 1].
    13	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    14	   1. CONSTANTS
    15	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    16	
    17	/** Default latency target in milliseconds. */
    18	const DEFAULT_LATENCY_TARGET_MS = 500;
    19	
    20	/** Weights for each component — must sum to 1.0. */
    21	const WEIGHTS = {
    22	  avgRelevance: 0.4,
    23	  topResult: 0.25,
    24	  countSaturation: 0.2,
    25	  latencyPenalty: 0.15,
    26	} as const;
    27	
    28	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    29	   2. TYPES
    30	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    31	
    32	/** Input parameters for the quality proxy computation. */
    33	export interface QualityProxyInput {
    34	  /**
    35	   * Average relevance score of returned results.
    36	   * Should be normalised to [0, 1] — typically avgRelevance / 3
    37	   * when raw relevance is on a 0-3 scale.
    38	   */
    39	  avgRelevance: number;
    40	
    41	  /**
    42	   * Relevance of the top result (rank 1).
    43	   * Should be normalised to [0, 1] — typically relevance / 3
    44	   * when raw relevance is on a 0-3 scale.
    45	   */
    46	  topResultRelevance: number;
    47	
    48	  /** Number of results actually returned by the search. */
    49	  resultCount: number;
    50	
    51	  /**
    52	   * Expected / desired number of results (e.g. the limit parameter
    53	   * passed to the search call).
    54	   * Must be > 0 to avoid division by zero; defaults to 1 when ≤ 0.
    55	   */
    56	  expectedCount: number;
    57	
    58	  /** Actual search latency in milliseconds. */
    59	  latencyMs: number;
    60	
    61	  /**
    62	   * Target latency in milliseconds.
    63	   * Defaults to 500 ms when omitted or ≤ 0.
    64	   */
    65	  latencyTargetMs?: number;
    66	}
    67	
    68	/** Breakdown of each component's weighted contribution. */
    69	export interface QualityProxyComponents {
    70	  /** avgRelevance × 0.40 */
    71	  avgRelevance: number;
    72	  /** topResultRelevance × 0.25 */
    73	  topResult: number;
    74	  /** countSaturation × 0.20 */
    75	  countSaturation: number;
    76	  /** latencyScore × 0.15 */
    77	  latencyPenalty: number;
    78	}
    79	
    80	/** Result of the quality proxy computation. */
    81	export interface QualityProxyResult {
    82	  /** Composite quality score in [0, 1]. */
    83	  score: number;
    84	  /** Weighted contribution of each individual component. */
    85	  components: QualityProxyComponents;
    86	  /** Human-readable quality tier: 'excellent' | 'good' | 'acceptable' | 'poor'. */
    87	  interpretation: 'excellent' | 'good' | 'acceptable' | 'poor';
    88	}
    89	
    90	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    91	   3. INTERNAL HELPERS
    92	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    93	
    94	/**
    95	 * Clamp a value to [min, max].
    96	 */
    97	function clamp(value: number, min: number, max: number): number {
    98	  return Math.min(max, Math.max(min, value));
    99	}
   100	
   101	/**
   102	 * Compute the count saturation component.
   103	 * Returns min(1, resultCount / expectedCount).
   104	 * When expectedCount ≤ 0 it is treated as 1 to avoid division by zero.
   105	 */
   106	function computeCountSaturation(
   107	  resultCount: number,
   108	  expectedCount: number,
   109	): number {
   110	  const safeExpected = expectedCount > 0 ? expectedCount : 1;
   111	  return Math.min(1, resultCount / safeExpected);
   112	}
   113	
   114	/**
   115	 * Compute the latency penalty component.
   116	 * Returns max(0, 1 − latencyMs / latencyTargetMs).
   117	 *   - 1.0 at latencyMs = 0 (perfect — full credit)
   118	 *   - 0.0 at latencyMs ≥ latencyTargetMs (at or over target)
   119	 * When latencyTargetMs ≤ 0 it is treated as DEFAULT_LATENCY_TARGET_MS.
   120	 */
   121	function computeLatencyScore(
   122	  latencyMs: number,
   123	  latencyTargetMs: number,
   124	): number {
   125	  const safeTarget =
   126	    latencyTargetMs > 0 ? latencyTargetMs : DEFAULT_LATENCY_TARGET_MS;
   127	  return Math.max(0, 1 - latencyMs / safeTarget);
   128	}
   129	
   130	/**
   131	 * Map a composite score to an interpretation label.
   132	 *   ≥ 0.8  → 'excellent'
   133	 *   ≥ 0.6  → 'good'
   134	 *   ≥ 0.4  → 'acceptable'
   135	 *   <  0.4 → 'poor'
   136	 */
   137	function interpretScore(
   138	  score: number,
   139	): 'excellent' | 'good' | 'acceptable' | 'poor' {
   140	  if (score >= 0.8) return 'excellent';
   141	  if (score >= 0.6) return 'good';
   142	  if (score >= 0.4) return 'acceptable';
   143	  return 'poor';
   144	}
   145	
   146	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   147	   4. CORE FUNCTION
   148	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   149	
   150	/**
   151	 * Compute the quality proxy score for a single search result set.
   152	 *
   153	 * All four components are normalised to [0, 1] before weighting:
   154	 *
   155	 *   avgRelevance    — direct pass-through (caller normalises to [0,1])
   156	 *   topResult       — direct pass-through (caller normalises to [0,1])
   157	 *   countSaturation — min(1, resultCount / expectedCount)
   158	 *   latencyPenalty  — max(0, 1 − latencyMs / latencyTargetMs)
   159	 *
   160	 * The final score is clamped to [0, 1] to guard against floating-point
   161	 * rounding errors.
   162	 *
   163	 * @returns QualityProxyResult with composite score, per-component
   164	 *          weighted contributions, and a human-readable interpretation.
   165	 */
   166	export function computeQualityProxy(
   167	  input: QualityProxyInput,
   168	): QualityProxyResult {
   169	  const {
   170	    avgRelevance,
   171	    topResultRelevance,
   172	    resultCount,
   173	    expectedCount,
   174	    latencyMs,
   175	    latencyTargetMs = DEFAULT_LATENCY_TARGET_MS,
   176	  } = input;
   177	
   178	  // Clamp raw inputs to [0, 1] — guard against out-of-range values
   179	  const clampedAvgRelevance = clamp(avgRelevance, 0, 1);
   180	  const clampedTopResult = clamp(topResultRelevance, 0, 1);
   181	
   182	  // Compute raw (unweighted) component values in [0, 1]
   183	  const rawCountSaturation = computeCountSaturation(resultCount, expectedCount);
   184	  const rawLatencyScore = computeLatencyScore(latencyMs, latencyTargetMs);
   185	
   186	  // Apply weights to produce each component's contribution
   187	  const components: QualityProxyComponents = {
   188	    avgRelevance: clampedAvgRelevance * WEIGHTS.avgRelevance,
   189	    topResult: clampedTopResult * WEIGHTS.topResult,
   190	    countSaturation: rawCountSaturation * WEIGHTS.countSaturation,
   191	    latencyPenalty: rawLatencyScore * WEIGHTS.latencyPenalty,
   192	  };
   193	
   194	  // Sum all weighted components and clamp to [0, 1]
   195	  const rawScore =
   196	    components.avgRelevance +
   197	    components.topResult +
   198	    components.countSaturation +
   199	    components.latencyPenalty;
   200	
   201	  const score = clamp(rawScore, 0, 1);
   202	
   203	  return {
   204	    score,
   205	    components,
   206	    interpretation: interpretScore(score),
   207	  };
   208	}
   209	
   210	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   211	   5. EXPORTS (re-export constants for test convenience)
   212	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   213	
   214	export { WEIGHTS, DEFAULT_LATENCY_TARGET_MS };
